TestCase z7Change(TestCase t1) {
    TestCase follow_case = t1;
    // --- Memory handling code generated based on the rule ---
    if (t1.a != NULL && t1.n1 > 0) {
        double* follow_case_a = (double*)malloc(t1.n1 * sizeof(double));
        if (follow_case_a != NULL) {
            memcpy(follow_case_a, t1.a, t1.n1 * sizeof(double));
            follow_case.a = follow_case_a;
        } else {
            // malloc failed, set follow_case.a to NULL to avoid accidental usage
            follow_case.a = NULL;
        }
    }
    // ------------------------------------

    // Defensive code generated based on rule (a) to prevent errors when n < 1 or a is NULL or malloc failed
    if (follow_case.a == NULL || follow_case.n1 <= 0) {
        // No elements to modify or memory not allocated, return as is.
        return follow_case;
    }

    // Specific operation code generated based on the rule
    // Collect indexes of elements less than 1
    int* candidates = (int*)malloc(follow_case.n1 * sizeof(int));
    if (candidates == NULL) {
        // malloc failed for candidates, skip modification
        return follow_case;
    }
    int candidate_count = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < follow_case.n1; i++) {
        if (follow_case.a[i] < 1.0) {
            candidates[candidate_count++] = i;
        }
    }
    if (candidate_count > 0) {
        // Seed the random number generator once
        static int seeded = 0;
        if (!seeded) {
            srand((unsigned int)time(NULL));
            seeded = 1;
        }
        int rand_index = candidates[rand() % candidate_count];
        follow_case.a[rand_index] = 1.0;
    }
    free(candidates);

    return follow_case;
}
